assessments.status_rationale: Today’s items discuss steps to increase Russian oil flows amid the Iran conflict, but provide no IEA statement/MOMR or member-country releases quantifying cumulative coordinated release volumes under a March 2026 plan.
News references elevated oil prices (around $100) and policy responses, but does not report an ICE Brent front-month settlement at or above $130 on any day.
No EIA weekly retail gasoline price data is cited in the provided news indicating prices at or above $4.25/gal for any week.

Articles describe Iran constricting/targeting the Strait of Hormuz and U.S. Navy escort plans, but do not provide UKMTO/IMB incident counts or confirm a 30-day window with 10+ distinct security incidents.
No Drewry World Container Index print level is reported in the provided items, so the $5,000/FEU threshold cannot be assessed.
Despite market-stress themed coverage (risk-off moves, fading rate-cut expectations), there is no official Fed (Board/FOMC) press release announcing a new or expanded liquidity backstop.
Market commentary suggests equity weakness and possible further declines, but the news does not document a specific day where the S&P 500 closed down 5.0% or more.

CNBC reports new Section 301 probes into 60 economies, but the prediction requires a USTR Federal Register notice proposing new/increased tariffs (≥10%) on Mexico and/or the EU covering ≥$50B in annual import value. The provided headline does not establish that such a tariff proposal notice has been published or that it meets the scope/coverage thresholds.
No SEC filing disclosure is cited showing Alphabet/Microsoft/Amazon/Meta/Apple raising 2026 capex guidance by ≥$10B specifically due to AI infrastructure/data centers.
No Oracle earnings release or 10-Q/10-K figure is provided indicating remaining performance obligations of at least $150B.
No ClinicalTrials.gov registration is referenced for a Phase 3 oral obstructive sleep apnea treatment trial with planned enrollment ≥500.
